mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month momentum in real activity-sensitive retail sales indicates improving household demand and near-term revenue expectations, causing higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS vintage value for 2020-07 was 535982, above the pandemic trough visible before 2020-08-15
failure_condition: Reject if 3-month RSAFS growth has Rank IC below 0.03 with 20-day S&P 500 returns over any 3-year rolling window, or positive RSAFS momentum does not raise hit rate above the unconditional 65.5% baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment is extremely elevated but begins falling, equities price a recovery in earnings before labor normalization is complete, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE > 8.0 and UNRATE declines by at least 0.3pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in less than 55% of cases or if their mean 60-day return is not higher than non-triggered high-unemployment observations at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A near-zero federal funds rate lowers discount rates and supports duration-sensitive equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when policy is pinned at the lower bound.
failure_condition: Reject if observations after FEDFUNDS crosses below 0.25 have 20-day S&P 500 returns below the unconditional mean or a positive-return frequency below 60% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in industrial production after a contraction signals restoration of operating leverage and supply-chain throughput, causing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change crosses from negative to positive
failure_condition: Reject if the first positive 3-month INDPRO change after contractions produces negative average 20-day S&P 500 returns or Rank IC below 0.03 across two consecutive 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll gains paired with a falling unemployment rate identify broad labor-market repair, improving income expectations and reducing default risk, causing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS vintage value for 2020-07 was 139582 and UNRATE vintage value was 10.2
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-up and UNRATE-down trigger does not outperform either single-series trigger by at least 1 percentage point in mean 60-day return in walk-forward samples.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices while the policy rate remains near zero compresses real short rates and shifts investors toward equities, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL vintage value for 2020-07 was 258.723 and FEDFUNDS was 0.09
regime_hint: financial repression with anchored policy rates
failure_condition: Reject if the CPIAUCSL-up and FEDFUNDS-low interaction has negative average 20-day returns or does not exceed the return of FEDFUNDS-low-only observations by at least 0.5 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts that rise sharply from depressed levels signal improving credit availability and durable-goods demand, leading cyclical earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST 3-month growth above 10% is followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in less than 58% of cases or its mean return is not above baseline at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A collapse in quarterly real GDP identifies severe earnings and balance-sheet stress; absent confirmation from higher-frequency demand data, it predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly annualized growth < -10% and RSAFS 1-month growth <= 0
regime_hint: acute demand shock without high-frequency demand confirmation
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases or if the conditional mean 5-day return is not below the unconditional mean in walk-forward testing.
